title: Preparing MEF-cultured hPSCs for nucleofection
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6hdh5","text":"This protocol describes the procedure or preparing MEF-cultured human pluripotent stem cells (hPSCs) for the delivery of plasmids, mRNA or ribonucleoprotein (RNP) using nucleofection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"74v49","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"gokv","text":"General notes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bqnh8","text":"1. Throughout this protocol, the term hPSC is used to collectively refer to both hiPSCs and hESCs. All described procedures have been tested and work equally well for hiPSCs and hESCs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9t6bu","text":"2. This protocol is to prepare cells for protocol nucleofection of hPSCs. Before starting, familiarize yourself with the protocol and the required preparations. A detailed protocol on maintaining MEF-cultured hPSCs can be found in the collection \"Maintenance and inactivation of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) as feeder cells for human pluripotent stem cell culture;\" doi:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8ksij","text":"3. Detailed protocols for preparing plasmids, RNA, and RNP for nucleofection can be found in the collection \"Nucleofection (Amaxa) and electroporation (Biorad) of hPSCs.\" A link to this collection can be found in the title section of this protocol, located above","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cctnj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f29bk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: When MEF-cultured hPSCs reach 50% confluency, change medium to hPSCs medium + Rock inhibitor, preparing for nucleofection the next day. For each 20 µl nucleofection reaction, prepare half to 1 well of cells. A detailed protocol on maintaining MEF-cultured hPSCs can be found in the collection "Maintenance and inactivation of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) as feeder cells for human pluripotent stem cell culture;" dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b4pbqvin

hPSCs medium %s %s DMEM/F12 385ml Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS) 75ml Knockout Serum Replacement 25ml L-Glutamine (100X) 5ml Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) 5ml MEM Non-Essential Amino Acids (100X) 5ml 2-Mercaptoethanol (10,000X) 50µl Heat Stable Recombinant Human FGF2 (25µg/ml) 80µl L-Glutamine (100X) %s %s L-Glutamine, powder 14.6 g MilliQ H2O 500 ml 2-Mercaptoethanol (10,000X) %s %s 2-Mercaptoethanol 0.78 ml MilliQ H2O 9.22 ml Heat Stable Recombinant Human FGF2 (25µg/ml) %s %s Heat Stable Recombinant Human FGF2 500 ug 0.1% BSA 20 ml Y-27632 (1,000X) %s %s Y-27632 5 mg DMSO 1.56ml hPSCs medium + Rock inhibitor, 500ml %s %s hESCs medium 500ml Y-27632 (1,000X) 500 µl

Prepare feeder plate at least 1 day earlier as depicted in the collection "Maintenance and inactivation of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) as feeder cell for human pluripotent stem cell culture," dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b4pbqvin
Wash MEF-cultured hPSCs with DPBS
Add 1 ml Dissociating Solution to each well
Collagenase solution (10mg/ml) %s %s Collagenase type IV 100 mg KSR medium 10ml
KSR medium %s %s DMEM/F12 385ml Knockout Serum Replacement 100ml L-Glutamine (200 mM) 5ml Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) 5ml MEM Non-Essential Amino Acids (100X) 5ml
Dissociating solution, 10ml %s %s Collagenase solution (10mg/ml) 1ml Dispase (1U/ml) 5ml DMEM/F12 4ml
Incubate 00:30:00 37 °C . Watch for edge curling of the colonies as indication collagenase incubation is complete.
Add 2 ml DMEM/F12 to each well
Pipette repeatedly with 5 ml pipette to lift colonies, careful not to carry over too many MEFs.
Collect into 15 ml conical tube.
Add 7 ml DMEM/F12.
Centrifuge at 200-300 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Aspirate supernatant
Re-suspend cell pellet in 1 ml pre-warmed Accutase
Incubate 37 °C 00:05:00
Add 9 ml DMEM/F12, invert to mix
Centrifuge at 200-300 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Aspirate supernatant
Resuspend cell pellet in 1 ml DMEM/F12, triturate to single cells using P1000 tips
Take two 10 µl sets of the cell suspension.. Mix each set with 10 µl trypan blue dye which comes with the Countess™ Cell Counting Chamber Slides

Count cells with Countess automated cell counter or hemocytometer, average the counts from the two sets. Continue with re-suspending the cell pellet in 20 µl nucleofection solution as described in the protocol "Nucleofection of hPSCs" (Step 2) The protocol "Nucleofection of hPSCs" can be found in the collection "Nucleofection (Amaxa) and electroporation (Biorad) of hPSCs." A link to this collection can be found in the title section of this protocol, located above
Mix the cell suspension in the conical tube, take 500,000 cells per nucleofection reaction and transfer to a new conical tube
Centrifuge at 200-300 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Aspirate supernatant
Resuspend cell pellet in 10 ml DPBS
Centrifuge at 200-300 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Aspirate supernatant as much as possible, to minimize the interference to the nucleofection buffer system.
To proceed with the nucleofection process, refer to the protocol "Nucleofection of hPSCs;" dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b4pcqviw
creator: Hanqin Li
